subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the story of Gopi and the magical paintbrush
Recognize the importance of kindness and helping others
Identify the main characters and events in the poem
Understand the conflict between Gopi and the Zamindar
Learn how creativity and courage can solve problems
Develop reading comprehension and vocabulary skills

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Magic and Imagination
student_activities: Discuss what they would draw if they had a magic paintbrush
Share examples of kindness they have seen or experienced

lesson_plan.title: Beginning of the Story
topics_covered: The mysterious man and the magic brush
The rule of using the brush for the poor
teaching_strategy: Teacher narration of the poem’s beginning
Guided reading of the first part of the poem
student_activities: Read the opening stanzas of the poem

lesson_plan.title: Gopi Helps the Villagers
topics_covered: Spread of the news about the magic
student_activities: List items Gopi painted for the villagers
Discuss how creativity can help people

lesson_plan.title: Conflict with the Zamindar
student_activities: Role-play the conversation between Gopi and the Zamindar
Explain why Gopi refused the Zamindar’s order

lesson_plan.title: Gopi’s Escape and Resolution
topics_covered: Gopi painting a road and a horse
teaching_strategy: Discussion of the climax and ending
Teacher explanation of the moral of the poem
student_activities: Explain how Gopi escaped from prison
Write the lesson learned from the story

teaching_aids: Illustrations of scenes from the poem
teacher_tip: Encourage students to imagine what they would draw if they had a magic brush. This activity helps students understand the theme of creativity and kindness in the story.
